ddls:
CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "identifier" INTEGER /* Identifier assigned to the participant in the study */,
  "constant" INTEGER /* Constant value (for internal use) */,
  "sex" INTEGER /* Sex of the participant (0 = Female, 1 = Male) */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the participant */,
  "nmarried" REAL /* Number of years married */,
  "nchildren" INTEGER /* Number of children */,
  "religious" INTEGER /* Religious belief (1 = Not Religious, 2 = Slightly Religious, 3 = Moderately Religious, 4 = Very Religious, 5 = Extremely Religious) */,
  "education" INTEGER /* Education level (years of education) */,
  "occupation" INTEGER /* Occupation (1 = Student, 2 = Farming/Unskilled, 3 = White Collar, 4 = Blue Collar, 5 = Professional, 6 = Manager, 7 = Other) */,
  "rate_marriage" INTEGER /* Marriage satisfaction rating (1 = Very Poor, 2 = Poor, 3 = Fair, 4 = Good, 5 = Very Good) */,
  "naffairs" INTEGER /* Number of extramarital affairs in the past year */,
  "relationship_status" TEXT /* Current relationship status (e.g., Married, Single, Divorced) */,
  "partner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant's partner, if applicable */,
  "communication_quality" INTEGER /* Quality of communication within the marriage (1 = Poor, 2 = Fair, 3 = Good, 4 = Very Good) */,
  "stress_level" INTEGER /* Current stress level of the participant (1 = Low, 2 = Moderate, 3 = High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race of the participant */,
  "income" REAL /* Annual income of the participant */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status (e.g., Married, Single, Divorced) */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the participant's residence */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Specific ethnic background of the participant */,
  "household_size" INTEGER /* Number of people living in the household */,
  "living_arrangement" TEXT /* Type of living arrangement (e.g., alone, with partner, with children) */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Current employment status (e.g., employed, unemployed, retired) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marriage_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marriage history record */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant */,
  "date_married" TEXT /* Date of marriage */,
  "date_divorced" TEXT /* Date of divorce (if applicable) */,
  "number_of_marriages" INTEGER /* Number of marriages the participant has had */,
  "marriage_duration" INTEGER /* Total duration of the marriage in years */,
  "reasons_for_divorce" TEXT /* Reasons for divorce if applicable */,
  "counseling_received" INTEGER /* Indicates if the participant has received marriage counseling */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marriage_history_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "affair_history" (
  "affair_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each affair record */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant */,
  "date_start" TEXT /* Date the affair started */,
  "date_end" TEXT /* Date the affair ended */,
  "affair_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the affair in months */,
  "affair_type" TEXT /* Type of affair (e.g., Emotional, Physical, Both) */,
  "affair_partner" INTEGER /* Number of affair partners */,
  "affair_reasons" TEXT /* Reasons for the affair */,
  "affair_impact" TEXT /* Impact of the affair on the marriage (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("affair_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_affair_history_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "satisfaction_history" (
  "satisfaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each satisfaction record */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant */,
  "date_rated" TEXT /* Date the satisfaction was rated */,
  "satisfaction_rating" INTEGER /* Marriage satisfaction rating (1 = Very Poor, 2 = Poor, 3 = Fair, 4 = Good, 5 = Very Good) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or additional notes about the rating */,
  "communication_quality_rating" INTEGER /* Quality of communication within the marriage (1 = Poor, 2 = Fair, 3 = Good, 4 = Very Good) */,
  "trust_rating" INTEGER /* Level of trust in the marriage (1 = Low, 2 = Moderate, 3 = High) */,
  "emotional_support_rating" INTEGER /* Level of emotional support in the marriage (1 = Low, 2 = Moderate, 3 = High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("satisfaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_satisfaction_history_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "follow_up_surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey record */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant */,
  "survey_date" TEXT /* Date the survey was conducted */,
  "survey_version" TEXT /* Version of the survey */,
  "marriage_satisfaction" INTEGER /* Marriage satisfaction rating (1 = Very Poor, 2 = Poor, 3 = Fair, 4 = Good, 5 = Very Good) */,
  "naffairs" INTEGER /* Number of extramarital affairs in the past year */,
  "nchildren" INTEGER /* Number of children */,
  "nmarried" REAL /* Number of years married */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or additional notes about the survey */,
  "stress_level" INTEGER /* Current stress level of the participant (1 = Low, 2 = Moderate, 3 = High) */,
  "communication_quality_rating" INTEGER /* Quality of communication within the marriage (1 = Poor, 2 = Fair, 3 = Good, 4 = Very Good) */,
  "trust_rating" INTEGER /* Level of trust in the marriage (1 = Low, 2 = Moderate, 3 = High) */,
  "emotional_support_rating" INTEGER /* Level of emotional support in the marriage (1 = Low, 2 = Moderate, 3 = High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_follow_up_surveys_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
